title: Authors and Citation
Authors and Citation
text: Piechota M (2026). rleops: Efficient Operations Run Length Encoded Vectors. R package version 0.0.1.
code: @Manual{,   title = {rleops: Efficient Operations on Run Length Encoded Vectors},   author = {Michal Piechota},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1}, }

what: Add two rle objects, element-wise — rle_add
title: Add two rle objects, element-wise — rle_add
Add two rle objects, element-wise — rle_add
code: rle_add(rle_a, rle_b, recycle = FALSE)

title: Add two rle objects, element-wise — rle_add
text: rle_a R rle list object rle_b R rle list object recycle TRUE, shorter rle recycled. FALSE, rle lengths must match.

title: Add two rle objects, element-wise — rle_add
text: R rle list object containing sum

title: Add two rle objects, element-wise — rle_add
code: rle_a <- list(lengths = c(2, 3), values = c(10, 20)) rle_b <- list(lengths = c(3, 2), values = c(5, 15)) result <- rle_add(rle_a, rle_b)  stopifnot(all.equal(result$lengths, c(2, 1, 2))) stopifnot(all.equal(result$values, c(15, 25, 35)))

what: Compact an rle object by merging identical runs — rle_compact
title: Compact an rle object by merging identical runs — rle_compact
text: Compact rle object merging identical runs

title: Compact an rle object by merging identical runs — rle_compact
code: rle_compact(rle)

title: Compact an rle object by merging identical runs — rle_compact
Compact an rle object by merging identical runs — rle_compact
text: R rle list object identical runs merged

title: Compact an rle object by merging identical runs — rle_compact
code: uncompacted <- list(lengths = c(2, 3, 1), values = c(5, 5, 10)) res1 <- rle_compact(uncompacted) stopifnot(all.equal(res1, list(lengths = c(5, 1), values = c(5, 10))))  compacted <- list(lengths = c(5, 2), values = c(1, 2)) stopifnot(all.equal(rle_compact(compacted), compacted))  nas <- list(lengths = c(1, 2), values = c(NA_real_, NA_real_)) res2 <- rle_compact(nas) stopifnot(all.equal(res2, list(lengths = 3, values = NA_real_)))

what: Cumulative Max of an rle object — rle_cummax
title: Cumulative Max of an rle object — rle_cummax
Cumulative Max of an rle object — rle_cummax
code: rle_cummax(rle)

title: Cumulative Max of an rle object — rle_cummax
Cumulative Max of an rle object — rle_cummax
Cumulative Max of an rle object — rle_cummax
code: rle_in <- list(lengths = c(2, 2, 2), values = c(5, 3, 7)) res1 <- rle_cummax(rle_in) stopifnot(all.equal(res1, list(lengths = c(4, 2), values = c(5, 7))))  rle_na1 <- list(lengths = c(2, 2), values = c(NA_real_, 5)) res2 <- rle_cummax(rle_na1) stopifnot(all.equal(res2, list(lengths = 4L, values = NA_real_)))  rle_na2 <- list(lengths = c(2, 2, 2), values = c(5, NA_real_, 10)) res3 <- rle_cummax(rle_na2) stopifnot(all.equal(res3, list(lengths = c(2L, 4L), values = c(5, NA_real_))))

what: Cumulative Min of an rle object — rle_cummin
title: Cumulative Min of an rle object — rle_cummin
Cumulative Min of an rle object — rle_cummin
code: rle_cummin(rle)

title: Cumulative Min of an rle object — rle_cummin
Cumulative Min of an rle object — rle_cummin
Cumulative Min of an rle object — rle_cummin
code: rle_in <- list(lengths = c(2, 2, 2), values = c(5, 8, 2)) res1 <- rle_cummin(rle_in) stopifnot(all.equal(res1, list(lengths = c(4, 2), values = c(5, 2))))  rle_na1 <- list(lengths = c(1, 2), values = c(NA_real_, -5)) res2 <- rle_cummin(rle_na1) stopifnot(all.equal(res2, list(lengths = 3L, values = NA_real_)))

what: Divide two rle objects, element-wise — rle_divide
title: Divide two rle objects, element-wise — rle_divide
Divide two rle objects, element-wise — rle_divide
code: rle_divide(rle_a, rle_b, recycle = FALSE)

title: Divide two rle objects, element-wise — rle_divide
text: rle_a R rle list object rle_b R rle list object recycle TRUE, shorter rle recycled. FALSE, rle lengths must match.

title: Divide two rle objects, element-wise — rle_divide
text: R rle list object containing quotient

title: Divide two rle objects, element-wise — rle_divide
code: rle_a <- list(lengths = c(2, 3), values = c(10, 20)) rle_b <- list(lengths = c(3, 2), values = c(5, 15)) result <- rle_divide(rle_a, rle_b)  stopifnot(all.equal(result$lengths, c(2, 1, 2))) stopifnot(all.equal(result$values, c(2, 4, 4/3)))

what: Element-wise equality of two rle objects — rle_eq
title: Element-wise equality of two rle objects — rle_eq
Element-wise equality of two rle objects — rle_eq
code: rle_eq(rle_a, rle_b, recycle = FALSE)

title: Element-wise equality of two rle objects — rle_eq
text: rle_a R rle list object rle_b R rle list object recycle TRUE, shorter rle recycled. FALSE, rle lengths must match.

title: Element-wise equality of two rle objects — rle_eq
text: R rle list object containing 1.0 (TRUE), 0.0 (FALSE) NaN

title: Element-wise equality of two rle objects — rle_eq
code: a <- list(lengths = c(2, 2), values = c(10, 20)) b <- list(lengths = c(2, 2), values = c(10, 99)) res1 <- rle_eq(a, b) stopifnot(all.equal(res1, list(lengths = c(2, 2), values = c(1.0, 0.0))))  c <- list(lengths = c(2), values = c(10)) d <- list(lengths = c(1, 1), values = c(NA_real_, 10)) res2 <- rle_eq(c, d) stopifnot(all.equal(res2, list(lengths = c(1, 1), values = c(NA_real_, 1.0))))

title: Filter an rle vector by another logical rle vector — rle_filter
code: rle_filter(rle_data, rle_mask)

text: rle_data R rle list object data filter rle_mask R rle list object logical mask

title: Filter an rle vector by another logical rle vector — rle_filter

what: Total length of the rle object (number of elements before encoding) — rle_length
title: Total length of the rle object (number of elements before encoding) — rle_length
text: Total length rle object (number elements encoding)

title: Total length of the rle object (number of elements before encoding) — rle_length
code: rle_length(rle)

title: Total length of the rle object (number of elements before encoding) — rle_length
Total length of the rle object (number of elements before encoding) — rle_length
Total length of the rle object (number of elements before encoding) — rle_length
code: rle_a <- list(lengths = c(2, 3, 1), values = c(1, 2, 3)) stopifnot(rle_length(rle_a) == 6)  empty <- list(lengths = integer(0), values = numeric(0)) stopifnot(rle_length(empty) == 0)

what: Mean of all values in an rle object — rle_mean
title: Mean of all values in an rle object — rle_mean
Mean of all values in an rle object — rle_mean
code: rle_mean(rle, na_rm = FALSE)

title: Mean of all values in an rle object — rle_mean
text: rle R rle list object na_rm TRUE, NA values ignored. Default FALSE.

title: Mean of all values in an rle object — rle_mean
Mean of all values in an rle object — rle_mean
code: rle_a <- list(lengths = c(2, 2), values = c(0.0, 4.0)) stopifnot(rle_mean(rle_a) == 2.0)  rle_na <- list(lengths = c(3, 1), values = c(6.0, NA_real_)) stopifnot(is.na(rle_mean(rle_na))) stopifnot(rle_mean(rle_na, na_rm = TRUE) == 6.0)

what: Multiply two rle objects, element-wise — rle_multiply
title: Multiply two rle objects, element-wise — rle_multiply
Multiply two rle objects, element-wise — rle_multiply
code: rle_multiply(rle_a, rle_b, recycle = FALSE)

title: Multiply two rle objects, element-wise — rle_multiply
text: rle_a R rle list object rle_b R rle list object recycle TRUE, shorter rle recycled. FALSE, rle lengths must match.

title: Multiply two rle objects, element-wise — rle_multiply
text: R rle list object containing product

title: Multiply two rle objects, element-wise — rle_multiply
code: rle_a <- list(lengths = c(2, 3), values = c(10, 20)) rle_b <- list(lengths = c(3, 2), values = c(5, 15)) result <- rle_multiply(rle_a, rle_b)  stopifnot(all.equal(result$lengths, c(2, 1, 2))) stopifnot(all.equal(result$values, c(50, 100, 300)))

what: Extract a slice of an rle vector — rle_slice
title: Extract a slice of an rle vector — rle_slice
Extract a slice of an rle vector — rle_slice
code: rle_slice(rle, start, end)

title: Extract a slice of an rle vector — rle_slice
text: rle R rle list object start 1-based starting index end 1-based ending index

title: Extract a slice of an rle vector — rle_slice
Extract a slice of an rle vector — rle_slice
code: rle_in <- list(lengths = c(3, 3, 3), values = c(10, 20, 30))  res1 <- rle_slice(rle_in, 2, 7) stopifnot((all.equal(res1, list(lengths = c(2, 3, 1), values = c(10, 20, 30)))))  res2 <- rle_slice(rle_in, 1, 9) stopifnot(all.equal(res2, rle_in))  res3 <- rle_slice(rle_in, 5, 5) stopifnot(all.equal(res3, list(lengths = 1, values = 20))) res4 <- rle_slice(rle_in, 8, 20) stopifnot(all.equal(res4, list(lengths = 2, values = 30)))

what: Substract two rle objects, element-wise — rle_substract
title: Substract two rle objects, element-wise — rle_substract
Substract two rle objects, element-wise — rle_substract
code: rle_substract(rle_a, rle_b, recycle = FALSE)

title: Substract two rle objects, element-wise — rle_substract
text: rle_a R rle list object rle_b R rle list object recycle TRUE, shorter rle recycled. FALSE, rle lengths must match.

title: Substract two rle objects, element-wise — rle_substract
text: R rle list object containing difference

title: Substract two rle objects, element-wise — rle_substract
code: rle_a <- list(lengths = c(2, 3), values = c(10, 20)) rle_b <- list(lengths = c(3, 2), values = c(5, 15)) result <- rle_substract(rle_a, rle_b)  stopifnot(all.equal(result$lengths, c(2, 1, 2))) stopifnot(all.equal(result$values, c(5, 15, 5)))

what: Sum of all values in an rle object — rle_sum
title: Sum of all values in an rle object — rle_sum
Sum of all values in an rle object — rle_sum
code: rle_sum(rle, na_rm = FALSE)

title: Sum of all values in an rle object — rle_sum
text: rle R rle list object na_rm TRUE, NA values ignored.

title: Sum of all values in an rle object — rle_sum
text: Sum values, NA value NA na_rm FALSE

title: Sum of all values in an rle object — rle_sum
code: rle_a <- list(lengths = c(2, 3), values = c(4.0, 2.0)) stopifnot(rle_sum(rle_a) == 14.0)  rle_na <- list(lengths = c(2, 1), values = c(3.0, NA_real_)) stopifnot(is.na(rle_sum(rle_na))) stopifnot(rle_sum(rle_na, na_rm = TRUE) == 6.0)

what: Unique values and their frequencies of an rle object — rle_value_counts
title: Unique values and their frequencies of an rle object — rle_value_counts
Unique values and their frequencies of an rle object — rle_value_counts
code: rle_value_counts(rle)

title: Unique values and their frequencies of an rle object — rle_value_counts
Unique values and their frequencies of an rle object — rle_value_counts
text: list containing unique values total counts

title: Unique values and their frequencies of an rle object — rle_value_counts
code: rle_in <- list(lengths = c(2, 3, 4), values = c(10, 20, 10)) res1 <- rle_value_counts(rle_in) # No guarantee on the order in output stopifnot(all.equal(res1$counts[res1$values == 10], 6)) stopifnot(all.equal(res1$counts[res1$values == 20], 3))  empty <- list(lengths = integer(0), values = numeric(0)) res3 <- rle_value_counts(empty) stopifnot(all.equal(length(res3$values), 0))

what: Find indices of TRUE (1.0) values in an rle object — rle_which
title: Find indices of TRUE (1.0) values in an rle object — rle_which
text: Find indices TRUE (1.0) values rle object

title: Find indices of TRUE (1.0) values in an rle object — rle_which
code: rle_which(rle)

title: Find indices of TRUE (1.0) values in an rle object — rle_which
text: rle R rle list object (1.0 represents TRUE)

title: Find indices of TRUE (1.0) values in an rle object — rle_which
Find indices of TRUE (1.0) values in an rle object — rle_which
code: rle_in <- list(lengths = c(2, 1, 2), values = c(0, 1, 1)) res1 <- rle_which(rle_in) stopifnot(all.equal(res1, c(3, 4, 5)))  rle_false <- list(lengths = c(5), values = c(0)) res2 <- rle_which(rle_false) stopifnot(all.equal(res2, integer(0)))  rle_na <- list(lengths = c(2, 1), values = c(NA_real_, 1)) res3 <- rle_which(rle_na) stopifnot(all.equal(res3, 3))
